title: 410 Gone follower cleanup deletes wrong rows when shared inbox is used
description: src/delivery.rs:227-230: When a delivery to a shared inbox gets HTTP 410, the code runs DELETE FROM followers WHERE inbox_uri = ? OR shared_inbox_uri = ? binding the same shared_inbox_uri value to both parameters. The first bind matches individual-inbox followers whose inbox_uri happens to equal the shared inbox URL (unlikely), not the followers who actually use shared_inbox_uri. The second bind compares shared_inbox_uri column against a shared inbox URL, which is correct, but only coincidentally. More critically: when fan-out chose shared_inbox_uri as the delivery target, the inbox_uri stored in delivery_queue.inbox_uri IS the shared inbox URL, so the WHERE clause should filter on shared_inbox_uri column = ?, not inbox_uri. Followers whose individual inboxes differ from the shared inbox URL are never cleaned up. Fix: on 410, look up which followers use that shared inbox and delete by shared_inbox_uri = ? only.
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
